verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation/palmitoylation — canonically cytoplasmic-facing. However, like other SFKs, LYN has documented ecto-display on tumor cells (cancer ecto-kinase pool) and surface exposure during apoptosis/immunogenic cell death contexts. Not lysosomal exocytosis; not germline-restricted; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering; no canonical PM-cycling surface pool in healthy cells. The cancer-cell ecto-kinase pool plus cell-death-associated externalization places it in cell_state_induced contextual rather than strict inner_leaflet_anchored no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength of evidence for LYN-specific (vs general SFK) ecto-display on tumor cells is uncertain; baseline is firmly inner-leaflet anchored.
